content.explanation: This example demonstrates different visual styles for the Button component using the variant prop, showcasing 6 variants ("solid", "subtle", "surface", "outline", "ghost", "plain"). Each variant provides a distinct visual appearance while maintaining the same functionality.
demonstrates: Using the variant prop to change Button visual style
Available variants: "solid", "subtle", "surface", "outline", "ghost", "plain"
keyPoints: The variant prop accepts: "solid", "subtle", "surface", "outline", "ghost", "plain"
Variants control visual appearance while preserving component functionality

content.explanation: This example demonstrates different visual styles for the Button component using the variant prop, showcasing 2 variants ("solid", "outline"). Each variant provides a distinct visual appearance while maintaining the same functionality.
content.code:
import { Button, HStack } from "@chakra-ui/react"
import { RiArrowRightLine, RiMailLine } from "react-icons/ri"

const Demo = () => {
  return (
    <HStack>
      <Button colorPalette="teal" variant="solid">
        <RiMailLine /> Email
      </Button>
      <Button colorPalette="teal" variant="outline">
        Call us <RiArrowRightLine />
      </Button>
    </HStack>
  )
}
demonstrates: Using the variant prop to change Button visual style
Available variants: "solid", "outline"
Combining variants with colorPalette for color theming
How variants work with other styling props
keyPoints: The variant prop accepts: "solid", "outline"
Variants control visual appearance while preserving component functionality
Variants can be combined with other props for customization

content.explanation: This example demonstrates different visual styles for the Button component using the variant prop, showcasing 3 variants ("outline", "surface", "subtle"). Each variant provides a distinct visual appearance while maintaining the same functionality.
content.code:
import { Button, Stack, Text } from "@chakra-ui/react"
import { colorPalettes } from "compositions/lib/color-palettes"

const Demo = () => {
  return (
    <Stack gap="2" align="flex-start">
      {colorPalettes.map((colorPalette) => (
        <Stack align="center" key={colorPalette} direction="row" gap="10">
          <Text minW="8ch">{colorPalette}</Text>
          <Button colorPalette={colorPalette}>Button</Button>
          <Button colorPalette={colorPalette} variant="outline">
            Button
          </Button>
          <Button colorPalette={colorPalette} variant="surface">
            Button
          </Button>
          <Button colorPalette={colorPalette} variant="subtle">
            Button
          </Button>
        </Stack>
      ))}
    </Stack>
  )
}
demonstrates: Using the variant prop to change Button visual style
Available variants: "outline", "surface", "subtle"
Combining variants with colorPalette for color theming
How variants work with other styling props
keyPoints: The variant prop accepts: "outline", "surface", "subtle"
Variants control visual appearance while preserving component functionality
Variants can be combined with other props for customization

content.explanation: This example demonstrates how to compose multiple components together, using 4 different components to create a cohesive interface. Component composition enables flexible and reusable UI patterns.
demonstrates: Composing multiple components together
Components used: Button, ButtonGroup, IconButton, LuChevronDown
How components work together to create complex UIs
keyPoints: Multiple components can be composed for complex interfaces
Component composition promotes reusability and maintainability
Import all components from the same package: package

content.explanation: This example demonstrates how to compose multiple components together, using 4 different components to create a cohesive interface. Component composition enables flexible and reusable UI patterns.
content.code:
import { Button, ButtonGroup, Stack, Text } from "@chakra-ui/react"

const Demo = () => {
  return (
    <Stack gap="8">
      <Stack>
        <Text textStyle="sm">Semantic Radius</Text>
        <ButtonGroup variant="subtle">
          <Button rounded="l1">Rounded l1</Button>
          <Button rounded="l2">Rounded l2</Button>
          <Button rounded="l3">Rounded l3</Button>
        </ButtonGroup>
      </Stack>

      <Stack>
        <Text textStyle="sm">Core Radius</Text>
        <ButtonGroup variant="subtle">
          <Button rounded="sm">Rounded sm</Button>
          <Button rounded="md">Rounded md</Button>
          <Button rounded="lg">Rounded lg</Button>
          <Button rounded="xl">Rounded xl</Button>
          <Button rounded="2xl">Rounded 2xl</Button>
          <Button rounded="full">Rounded full</Button>
        </ButtonGroup>
      </Stack>
    </Stack>
  )
}
demonstrates: Composing multiple components together
Components used: Button, ButtonGroup, Stack, Text
How components work together to create complex UIs
keyPoints: Multiple components can be composed for complex interfaces
Component composition promotes reusability and maintainability
Import all components from the same package: package
